campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they honored K9 Lucas, a police dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including updates to beekeeping rules, a new transit-oriented development bill that requires some council members to step aside due to conflicts of interest, and a study about potentially creating regional homeless services in the West Valley area.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial and recognition
Beekeeping ordinance updated and approved
Senate Bill 79 transit development hearing (conflicts of interest noted)
West Valley regional homeless services study and task force
City financial report for 2025 accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a special meeting to review and approve the work plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and recreation to traffic safety and planning. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also discussed a legal matter in a private session.
keyTopics: Parks and recreation priorities for 2026-27
Heritage preservation and public art initiatives
Library services and programming
Traffic safety projects and concerns
Planning commission development priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving previous meeting notes and the city's financial report. The council also adopted policies on weed control completion, established new meeting procedures, approved housing affordability rules, and agreed to raise two new flags—one for Pride Month and one for Juneteenth.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion and final report
Below Market Rate housing unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
New City Council meeting norms and procedures
VTA transportation presentation and housing element planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and move forward on several projects including a new housing development called Solana. The council also discussed plans for community health and senior services, and reviewed progress on the city's long-term planning goals.
keyTopics: Solana Project housing development - final approval for Tract 10657
January 2026 financial report and investment updates
General Plan and Housing Element progress check
Community Health and Senior Services work plan for 2026
Updates to city council meeting minute procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed which items to add to each meeting, set specific times for certain discussion topics, and checked legal notice requirements for any agenda items or documents.
keyTopics: Finalized April 7 meeting agenda items and scheduling
Planned April 14 meeting draft agenda items
Set specific time slots for agenda discussions
Reviewed legal notice requirements for transparency

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed committee reports and upcoming agendas for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comments and oral communications
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community services in Sunnyvale. The group approved notes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations asking for funding support.
keyTopics: Federal housing grants (CDBG) being distributed to local organizations
Applications from groups seeking funding for housing and human services
February meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses succeed by launching programs to get them registered and involved in the community. They discussed plans to promote shopping locally through summer events and partnerships with shopping centers, plus new incentives to attract retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help unregistered businesses get compliant
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local campaign to boost local spending
Public-private partnerships to sponsor city events
Economic incentives to attract retail and startup businesses to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city will review and discuss buildings or sites with historical importance. Residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom, and can submit written comments about the projects being reviewed.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting via email
Zoom link: https://santaclaraca.zoom.us/j/97233262035 or phone (669) 900-6833

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 17, 2026 to handle routine administrative business, including reviewing meeting schedules and approving previous meeting notes. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to the WELL 2026 conference and confirmed a new representative to serve on the Economic Development board for the school district.
keyTopics: Approved Mayor Montano's travel to WELL 2026 Annual Conference
Confirmed new Milpitas Unified School District representative for Economic Development position
Reviewed and approved previous council meeting minutes
Set calendar for upcoming council meetings
Reviewed member-requested agenda items for future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several routine items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters and authorized a purchase of email security software (Mimecast) for $93,383 per year over three years.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
